artifacts.name: openclaw-twitter
rule_summaries: OAuth, posting, or media-upload side effects are not explicit enough.
recommended_actions: Explain user approval, upload paths, and posting behavior in publish-facing docs.
Avoid phrasing that makes posting look silent or automatic by default.
artifacts.reason: The skill can perform public Twitter/X posting and is instructed to try publishing directly when posting intent is detected.

artifacts.name: prediction-market
rule_summaries: Trust and provenance surfaces are weaker than the package behavior requires.
recommended_actions: Keep source links, manifests, and README provenance aligned.
Make external writes, uploads, and remote execution boundaries easy to verify.
artifacts.reason: The skill provides raw on-chain betting transactions and weak project provenance for a real-money market.

artifacts.name: stock-analysis
rule_summaries: Relay/API trust surface is under-described for the shipped package.
recommended_actions: State the network target and secret flow explicitly in SKILL.md, README, and manifests.
Keep the auth story API-key-only unless the runtime truly needs more.
artifacts.reason: Optional third-party CLI installation and user-configured cron automation are disclosed and purpose-aligned, but users should review them before enabling.

artifacts.name: web-search
rule_summaries: Trust and provenance surfaces are weaker than the package behavior requires.
recommended_actions: Keep source links, manifests, and README provenance aligned.
Make external writes, uploads, and remote execution boundaries easy to verify.
artifacts.reason: This skill should be used when users need to search the web for information, find current content, look up news articles, search for images, or find videos. It uses DuckDuckGo's search API to return results in clean, formatted output (text, markdown, or JSON). Use for research, fact-checking, finding recent information, or gathering web resources.

artifacts.name: x-intelligence-automation
rule_summaries: Registry/manifest/docs/env requirements drifted away from the shipped runtime.
OAuth, posting, or media-upload side effects are not explicit enough.
Trust and provenance surfaces are weaker than the package behavior requires.
Relay/API trust surface is under-described for the shipped package.
recommended_actions: Align SKILL.md, README, package manifests, and registry-facing metadata.
Declare every required env var in the same shape across all publish layers.
Explain user approval, upload paths, and posting behavior in publish-facing docs.
Avoid phrasing that makes posting look silent or automatic by default.
Keep source links, manifests, and README provenance aligned.
Make external writes, uploads, and remote execution boundaries easy to verify.
State the network target and secret flow explicitly in SKILL.md, README, and manifests.
Keep the auth story API-key-only unless the runtime truly needs more.
artifacts.reason: The skill is broadly coherent with a Twitter/X automation client using the AIsa relay, but there are a few mismatches you should be aware of before installing (undeclared env overrides and uploading local files to an external relay).
